pi_items.source: NREL Forum discussion - BModes analysis with structural pretwist for NREL 5MW blade. The exact value from BModes is 0.6958 Hz, which rounds to approximately 0.70 Hz. Reference: https://forums.nrel.gov/t/eigenfrequencies-of-nrel-5mw-blade-full-system/2282

Derived from NREL 5MW specifications. Task requires increasing baseline frequency (~0.70 Hz) to 0.75 Hz, which is between the BModes result (0.6958 Hz) and the Sandia 3D ANSYS reference (0.87 Hz). This is a realistic design improvement target that avoids resonance issues. Reference: https://forums.nrel.gov/t/eigenfrequencies-of-nrel-5mw-blade-full-system/2282
Task specification in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh. Path follows standard QBlade project file convention (.wpa extension). Unverified against actual filesystem but consistent with task design.
Task specification in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh. Path is reasonable and consistent with task output directory structure.
Pattern is standard for locating NREL 5MW sample projects in QBlade. Used in setup_task.sh to find baseline project for md5 comparison.

privileged_info_summary: The NREL 5MW blade baseline first flapwise eigenfrequency is approximately 0.6958 Hz (rounds to ~0.70 Hz) when calculated using BModes with structural pretwist, as confirmed by NREL forum discussions and technical documentation. The target frequency of 0.75 Hz is a reasonable design improvement objective, positioned between the baseline (~0.70 Hz) and the Sandia reference value (0.87 Hz). The task description accurately states the baseline should be 'around 0.6-0.7 Hz,' which is verified. The task metadata frequencies are based on legitimate NREL technical references and are consistent with published wind turbine structural analysis data.
